chunks.text: Joshua Hug is an Associate Teaching Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office location at 779 Soda Hall, email hug@cs.berkeley.edu, and fax 510-642-5775. Office hours are Monday 11-12 in 779 Soda and Friday 2-3 in 210 Wheeler.

Joshua Hug's education includes a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 2011 and a B.S. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from University of Texas at Austin in 2003. His research areas focus on Education (EDUC) and Generative Art.

Joshua Hug teaches CS 47B: Completion of Work in Computer Science 61B and CS 61B: Data Structures, which meets MoWeFr 13:00-13:59 in Wheeler 150. His research support is provided by Natalie Chen, contactable at nchen86@berkeley.edu.

Joshua Hug has received several teaching awards: UC Berkeley Distinguished Teaching Award in 2023, Jim and Donna Gray Award for Excellence in Undergraduate Teaching of Computer Science in 2018, and Diane S. McEntyre Award for Excellence in Teaching Computer Science in 2017.
